subject: UX & Research
title: Error Message Language
description: A fast breakdown of Error Message Language for builders.

hook.text: 'Error 403: Forbidden.' A user stares at this and has no idea what they did wrong or how to fix it.

buildup.text: Error messages are the most critical microcopy in any product. They appear when users are already frustrated.

discovery.text: Good error messages explain three things: what happened, why it happened, and what to do next.

twist.text: Slack says 'You've been signed out. Your session probably expired. Sign in again.' Human, clear, actionable.

climax.text: Never blame the user. 'Invalid input' sounds like their fault. 'Please enter a valid email address' guides them forward.

punchline.text: Good errors explain, guide, and never blame. Bad errors confuse and frustrate.

quiz.question: What three elements should every good error message include?
options: What happened, why it happened, and what the user should do next
An error code, a timestamp, and a support link
An apology, a joke, and a retry button
